title: phase_executor Observation
description: CURRENT schema_version 2 of the Observation contract. Normative, language-neutral record of one model seat invocation. contract.py is one producer; a Rust producer (kukakuka) emits the same documents. actual_model + usage are mandatory evidence when parse_status == 'ok' (absent identity/usage on a successful call is a failure, not an unknown success), and MAY be null only for a non-success parse_status so a pre-envelope timeout is still recordable. v2 adds the optional-additive work_product object (executor-derived git evidence) + the AC-I1 telemetry fields (session_policy, worktree_id, tmux_session, budget, hook_denials). Versioning policy: a field whose addition breaks an older vendored copy (any change under additionalProperties:false) bumps schema_version. Each version ships as its own FROZEN observation-<n>.schema.json and is never edited after release. A document is validated against the schema of its DECLARED schema_version (unknown version = fail-closed). New producers emit the current version; prior-version documents are never retro-mutated.

schema_version.description: Observation schema version. Bump on any change that breaks an older vendored copy (any addition under additionalProperties:false). A document is validated against the schema of this DECLARED version; unknown version = fail-closed.

correlation_id.description: Opaque caller-supplied string (rawgentic: WF2 step/task id; kukakuka: room turn nonce). Optional.

engine.description: Provider adapter family, e.g. claude | codex | zhipuai. Open string so downstream projects can add engines.

transport.description: How the engine was reached, e.g. native | ccr. A proxy hop is auditable here plus the innermost upstream id in actual_model.

actual_model.description: Provider-reported id from the INNERMOST envelope. MUST be a non-empty string when parse_status == 'ok'; MAY be null only for a non-success parse_status.

context_hashes.description: Hashes of each context input, in order. Empty array when no extra context.

usage.description: Real provider-reported token counts. Non-null with input+output present when parse_status == 'ok'; MAY be null only for a non-success parse_status.

parse_status.description: ok = success with provider evidence. All others are non-success: actual_model/usage MAY be null. usage_unavailable = the call produced output and an identity but the provider surfaced no usage counts (a named degraded state, never a fabricated zero).

parsed_payload.description: The parsed provider output (structured object, text, or null on a pre-payload failure).

raw_capture_path.description: Path to the capture directory holding the raw transport bytes. Null only if capture was not written (harness/launch error before mkdir).

fallback_reason.description: Why a fallback chain entry was used, or null when the primary served.

judge_degraded.description: Competitive-round flag: the judge failed and a degraded winner policy was applied. Optional.

dispatched_lane.description: The lane the executor ACTUALLY dispatched on (#425 B), stamped by the engine at dispatch — the SAME shape as a routing-table $defs/lane (same required set + all 6 optional-inclusive properties, incl. participation_mode) so a lane can be stamped verbatim without projection. OPTIONAL/additive (a v1 producer that omits it still validates; kukakuka parity). Enables independent receipt<->observation binding in the run-end audit (enforce.reconcile_run).

routing_config_digest.description: Digest of the immutable routing snapshot that served this call (epoch identity).

effort.description: #465: the effort resolution used for this dispatch (optional-additive; absent on legacy records). requested = normalized level asked for (null = none requested); native = value sent on the wire (null = provider default); resolution = how native was chosen.

canary_result.description: #468 W5: the fail-closed guardrail-canary PASS summary for a dispatched launch (a refusal never spawns, so this is always a pass). EXACTLY the keys canary.CanaryResult.pass_summary() emits (a test pins the two sets equal). OPTIONAL/additive (absent on legacy/non-canary records); refusal data travels on the CompositionError, never here.

work_product.description: #469 W6 (OQ-4): what an agentic seat PRODUCED, EXECUTOR-DERIVED (never the agent's self-report). All git evidence (base/head/content-tree SHA, changed_paths) is derived by the executor from the trusted worktree gitdir via contract.derive_work_product; a provider's self-reported claim stays only in parsed_payload and never feeds this object. content_tree_sha is the anti-tamper CONTENT commitment: the git tree object id of the FULL worktree state (committed + dirty + untracked) under one snapshot boundary, so a dirty/untracked byte change is caught even when head_sha + changed_path names are unchanged. OPTIONAL/additive (absent on legacy/read-only records) — emitted only when set. Population of the dispatch-time fields is wired by #470.

kind.description: Governed work-product category (the deliverable class the seat produced), distinct from the open-string top-level `seat`. A new category is a breaking add for an old vendored copy — it bumps schema_version.

worktree_path.description: Absolute path of the engine-managed worktree the product was derived from (host-specific; redaction/retention is defined on the consuming run-record / #473 sidecar, not here).

base_sha.description: The commit the worktree was cut at (handle.base_sha) — executor-derived.

head_sha.description: The worktree HEAD commit (rev-parse via the trusted gitdir) — executor-derived. NOT a content commitment on its own (a dirty/untracked file's bytes can change while head_sha holds); content_tree_sha is the content commitment.

content_tree_sha.description: git tree object id of the FULL worktree state (committed + dirty + untracked, .gitignore-respecting) written under one executor-controlled snapshot boundary — the anti-tamper CONTENT commitment; changed_paths is derived from this SAME tree.

changed_paths.description: Worktree-relative paths differing from base under the content_tree_sha snapshot (sorted, unique). Empty for a no-change seat. Executor-derived from the same candidate tree as content_tree_sha — never an agent-reported path list.

documents.description: Executor-VERIFIED produced documents — restricted to entries within changed_paths (an out-of-worktree report uses a test entry's report_ref, not a git-evidence claim). Empty when none.

tests.description: Executor-observed test runs (never parsed from the agent payload). Empty when none ran.

command_digest.description: Digest of the executor-observed test command.

exit_code.description: Process exit code, or null when the command never produced one (pre-launch/timeout).

report_ref.description: Reference to the test report artifact (a path or opaque ref scheme; used for out-of-worktree reports).

promotion_status.description: Whether/how promotion ran for this product. A work_product exists for failed/rejected/never-promoted seats too (not_attempted). A supplied PromotionResult is reconciled against the derived evidence, never copied.

session_policy.description: #469 W6 (AC-I1): the D-8 session policy actually used for this dispatch. OPTIONAL/additive (absent on legacy/read-only records) — POPULATION is wired by #470.

worktree_id.description: #469 W6 (AC-I1): the WorktreeIdentity of the engine-managed worktree for this dispatch. OPTIONAL/additive — POPULATION is wired by #470.

tmux_session.description: #469 W6 (AC-I1): the supervisor tmux session name (registry.session_name) for this dispatch. Host-specific; redaction/retention is defined on the consuming run-record / #473 sidecar, not here. OPTIONAL/additive — POPULATION is wired by #470.

budget.description: #469 W6 (AC-I1): the cost envelope for this dispatch. Unit is US dollars; values carry cent precision (a value is USD, e.g. 0.25 = 25 cents). OPTIONAL/additive — POPULATION is wired by #470.

spent_usd.description: USD actually spent by this dispatch (>= 0).

hook_denials.description: #469 W6 (AC-I1): count of PreToolUse hook denials the seat received during this dispatch (a nonnegative COUNT — detailed denial EVENTS go to the run-record, not every Observation, avoiding duplicated/sensitive payloads per-dispatch). OPTIONAL/additive — POPULATION is wired by #470.
